item.title: Top Japan firms set to offer big pay hikes, focus on Iran conflict ahead
item.description: Many major companies are set to offer big pay hikes as they wrap up their annual wage ‚Äãtalks with unions on Wednesday, reflecting strong pay momentum ‚Äåfor a fourth consecutive year, although uncertainty from the Middle East conflict clouds the outlook.

item.title: BP locks out union workers at its Midwest refinery
item.description: BP on Tuesday barred about 800 ‚ÄãUnited Steelworkers members ‚Äåfrom its 440,000 barrel-per-day refinery in Whiting, ‚ÄãIndiana starting at ‚Äã12:00 a.m. on March ‚ÅÝ19 after negotiations ‚Äãwith the union broke ‚Äãdown.

item.title: Exclusive: Nvidia preparing Groq chips that can be sold in Chinese market, sources say
item.description: Nvidia is preparing a ‚Äãversion of its Groq artificial-intelligence chips that can be sold to ‚Äåthe Chinese market, two sources familiar with the matter told Reuters on Tuesday.

item.title: US Postal Service could run out of money as soon as October
item.description: The head of the U.S. Postal Service told Congress on Tuesday it could run out of ‚Äãmoney in October or November if the agency continues to make required ‚Äåretirement and other payments to the government.

item.title: Amazon plans drastic cut in packages it sends through post office, WSJ reports
item.description: Amazon.com is looking to sharply cut the ‚Äãnumber of packages it ‚Äåsends through the U.S. Postal Service, a move that ‚Äãcould cost the agency ‚Äãmillions of dollars in revenue, ‚ÅÝthe Wall Street ‚ÄãJournal reported on Tuesday.

item.title: Australia's Woodside names Liz Westcott as new CEO
item.description: Australia's ‚ÄãWoodside ‚ÄåEnergy ‚Äãnamed ‚ÄãLiz ‚ÅÝWestcott ‚Äãas ‚Äãits new ‚Äãchief ‚Äãexecutive officer ‚Äåand ‚ÅÝmanaging ‚Äãdirector ‚Äãon ‚ÅÝWednesday.

item.title: FTC monitoring how drug companies react to patent cliff, official says
item.description: The ‚ÄãU.S. Federal Trade Commission is on the lookout for anticompetitive practices in the pharmaceutical ‚Äåindustry with a number of blockbuster drugs near the end of their patents, the FTC's head of antitrust enforcement said on Tuesday, as the agency looks to protect consumers from unfair prices.

item.title: US gasoline prices soar past $3.75 a gallon as Middle East war rages on
item.description: The U.S. national average price for gasoline surpassed $3.75 a gallon for the first time since October ‚Äã2023 on Tuesday, GasBuddy data showed, as global fuel ‚Äåmarkets continue to reel under supply disruptions caused by the Middle East war.

item.title: BHP names Brandon Craig as CEO, succeeding Mike Henry
item.description: Mining giant BHP ‚ÄãGroup on Wednesday named Brandon Craig as ‚ÄåCEO to succeed Mike Henry, who will step down after more than six years at ‚Äãthe helm.

item.title: Trading Day: Oil back above $100... and so?
item.description: Stocks rose, and bond yields and the dollar slipped on Tuesday, as investors shrugged off a rebound in oil prices that lifted Brent crude back above $100 a barrel, and turned their attention to the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision on Wednesday.

item.title: US securities regulator issues long-awaited crypto guidance
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Tuesday issued an interpretation clarifying which types of cryptocurrencies are considered securities and how a "non-security" ‚Äãdigital asset could meet certain conditions to become an investment contract.

item.title: Lululemon sees annual revenue, profit below estimates amid board, demand challenges
item.description: Athletic apparel ‚Äãmaker Lululemon forecast 2026 revenue ‚Äåand profit below Wall Street expectations on Tuesday, underscoring its challenges as it faces ‚Äãrising competition and an attempt ‚Äãby founder Chip Wilson to overhaul ‚ÅÝthe board.

item.title: Nvidia sales opportunity for Blackwell, Rubin chips more than $1 trillion by 2027
item.description: Nvidia CEO ‚ÄãJensen Huang said ‚Äåon Tuesday at a press conference ‚Äãthat the revenue ‚Äãopportunity for the company's ‚ÅÝBlackwell and ‚ÄãRubin artificial intelligence ‚Äãchips is "likely to be larger than $1 trillion" by ‚Äãthe end ‚Äãof 2027. The estimate does ‚Äånot ‚ÅÝinclude the company's networking chips and the new ‚Äãprocessors made ‚Äãwith ‚ÅÝtechnology from the Groq ‚Äãlicensing deal it ‚Äãsigned ‚ÅÝin December.

item.title: Exclusive: Amazon CEO sees AI doubling prior AWS sales projections to $600 billion by 2036
item.description: Amazon O CEO Andy Jassy ‚Äãsaid during an internal all-hands meeting ‚Äåhe expects artificial intelligence could help cloud computing unit Amazon Web Services achieve $600 billion in annual sales, ‚Äãdouble his own prior estimate.

item.title: Elon Musk, US SEC in talks to settle lawsuit over Twitter disclosures
item.description: Elon Musk and the ‚ÄãU.S. Securities and Exchange Commission are in ‚Äåtalks to settle the regulator's lawsuit accusing the world's richest person of waiting too long to ‚Äãdisclose his purchases of Twitter shares ‚Äãin 2022.

item.title: Senator wants White House to answer if TikTok investors will pay $10 billion to US Treasury
item.description: The top Democrat on the Senate Intelligence ‚ÄãCommittee on Tuesday asked ‚Äåthe White House to answer if investors are paying $10 ‚Äãbillion to the ‚ÄãTreasury as part of the ‚ÅÝTrump administration-brokered sale ‚Äãof TikTok‚Äôs U.S. operations.

item.title: Big drugmakers must face US overcharge claims on medications for low-income patients
item.description: A U.S. appeals court on ‚ÄãTuesday revived a whistleblower lawsuit accusing four large drugmakers of ‚Äådefrauding the federal and state governments out of hundreds of millions of dollars by overcharging on medications for low-income and uninsured patients.

item.title: Unicredit should clarify intentions for Commerzbank, German state premier says
item.description: The premier of the German state of Hesse on ‚ÄãTuesday called on Italy's UniCredit ‚Äåto submit a concrete offer for the German lender Commerzbank and to clarify ‚Äãits objectives.

item.title: Analysts revise AI hyperscaler debt forecasts after Amazon bond sale
item.description: Analysts anticipate a higher supply of debt being raised by ‚Äãthe Big Five hyperscaler companies this year as they race to build out their data center ‚Äåinfrastructure, following Amazon's near-record bond sale last week of roughly $54 billion in investment-grade bonds.

item.title: UBS faced technology outage that impacted trading business, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: UBS Group has ‚Äãbeen working ‚Äåto fix a ‚Äãglobal ‚Äãtechnology outage that impacted ‚ÅÝsome ‚Äãof its ‚Äãtrading business, Bloomberg News reported ‚Äãon ‚ÄãTuesday.

item.title: US stock market crash fears ease even as Middle East war rages on
item.description: Options traders' fears of a U.S. stock market crash have pulled ‚Äåback nearly to levels seen before the U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran that made oil prices soar.

item.title: U.S. Democratic lawmakers introduce bill to crack down on prediction markets
item.description: U.S. Democratic lawmakers Senator Chris Murphy and Representative Greg Casar on Tuesday introduced ‚Äãa bill to ban prediction market bets ‚Äåon military operations and other sensitive government actions, increasing pressure on the fast-growing markets.

item.title: Baidu joins China's OpenClaw frenzy with new AI agents
item.description: Chinese tech giant Baidu on Tuesday unveiled a suite of artificial intelligence products, tapping growing domestic interest in OpenClaw, an open-source framework ‚Äãfor agents that can perform complex tasks with less human input than ‚Äåchatbots.

item.title: Kalshi criminally¬Ýcharged in Arizona for operating illegal gambling business
item.description: Arizona's attorney general on Tuesday filed criminal charges against Kalshi, accusing the prediction market platform of operating an illegal gambling ‚Äãbusiness in the state and unlawfully allowing people to place bets on elections.

item.title: Honeywell, Howmet juggle rising defense demand and commercial aerospace boom
item.description: Executives from Honeywell Aerospace and Howmet Aerospace ‚Äãsaid on Tuesday they could grow capacity to meet strong commercial demand for ‚Äåplane parts and munitions as the defense sector ramps up due to geopolitical strife.

item.title: Venezuela's PDVSA ratifies Asdrubal Chavez as head of US units including Citgo
item.description: The board of directors of Venezuela's state-run PDVSA this week ‚Äãratified Asdrubal Chavez as head ‚Äåof the oil company's U.S. subsidiaries PDV Holding, Citgo Holding and Citgo Petroleum, ‚Äãa document seen by Reuters showed, ‚Äãa decision that follows U.S. President ‚ÅÝDonald Trump's support of interim ‚ÄãPresident Delcy Rodriguez's administration.

item.title: Microsoft unifies Copilot commercial and consumer product teams in unit rejig
item.description: Microsoft said on Tuesday it ‚Äãis reorganizing its ‚ÄåCopilot AI product teams by unifying ‚Äãits commercial ‚Äãand consumer versions, as ‚ÅÝthe tech ‚Äãgiant rushes to ‚Äãimprove its AI assistant product and drive better ‚Äãadoption.

item.title: GM, LG retool Tennessee battery plant for energy storage batteries, recall laid-off workers
item.description: General Motors and battery partner LG ‚ÄãEnergy Solution are transforming an electric-vehicle battery plant in Tennessee to ‚Äãmake batteries for energy storage systems, the companies said Tuesday.

item.title: Boeing shareholders can pursue class action over 737 MAX safety
item.description: A federal judge ‚Äãcertified a shareholder class action accusing Boeing of concealing safety deficiencies in ‚Äåits 737 MAX planes before two crashes that killed 346 people in 2018 and 2019.

item.title: Debt investors offloading exposure to software companies is latest sign of pain
item.description: Investors are offloading software loans in debt vehicles at a discount, in the latest sign of pain in the software industry, which is being upended by AI.
